title: Dinas Camp near Harlech (half-term)
summary: A half-term camping holiday in May 1989 at Dinas Camp near Harlech in north Wales, with all six children including baby Charles (6 weeks) and toddler John. Day trips from the fixed camp base included Harlech Castle, a sandy beach, a river swim, and a picnic at the Roman Steps in the Rhinog Mountains.

title: Dinas Camp near Harlech (July, with family)
summary: A family camping holiday at Dinas Camp near Harlech in North Wales, joining friends Tim, Margaret, Helen, David, Sally, Maia and Grandma. The family enjoyed fine weather with outdoor meals, hill walks, a river swim, and a day trip to Porthmadog, stopping at the Centre for Alternative Technology on the drive home.

title: Malvern picnic & Kate & Jamie's farm
summary: A short domestic trip in May 1989 beginning with a day picnic at Malvern including a visit to a quilting exhibition, followed by one night at the farm of friends Kate and Jamie. The family's new van was used and the boys played on bikes and a water slide.

title: Northern tour: Leeds, York, Hadrian's Wall, Settle, Burnley, Warburton & Arley Hall
summary: An eight-day family camping tour of northern England in August 1989, starting from Birmingham on John's birthday. They camped at the Sykes' in Leeds, then a Bishopthorpe campsite for visiting York (Minster, Shambles, Castle Museum), then Stonehaugh for Hadrian's Wall (Chesters, Hexham, Vindolanda, Housesteads), then Wood End campsite near Austwick/Settle for walks around Ribblesdale. On the way home they stopped at Mr. Rourke's wrought-iron factory in Burnley, St Werburgh's church at Warburton, and Arley Hall in Cheshire.

title: Peak District weekend (Chatsworth, Alport, Youlgreave)
summary: A weekend trip in July 1989 to the Peak District, Derbyshire. The family slept in the van at Hopping Farm near Alport, walked to the villages of Alport and Youlgreave, and visited Chatsworth House with its Capability Brown landscape. Haddon Hall was closed on the day.

title: Stokesay Castle, Shropshire (last trip of the year)
summary: A one-night autumn trip to Shropshire on 28–29 October 1989, billed as the last camping trip of the year. The family drove from Birmingham via Astley Abbots (calling on Natalie Hodgson, who was out) to visit Stokesay Castle in the rain, then stayed overnight at Hamperley campsite. The following morning they visited Ludlow Castle before returning home.

title: Ardèche (France) & the Wegners in W. Germany
summary: In late July 1990 Simon and Eve took Thomas, William and Peter (John and Charles stayed home) by camper van from Birmingham across the Dover–Calais ferry (Pride of Calais), down through Reims, Arc-en-Barrois, Dijon and Lyon to a long fixed-base holiday in the Ardèche, swimming and canoeing the Ardèche and Chassezac gorges around Casteljau and St-Martin-d'Ardèche/Aiguèze. They then drove north via Thionville and Trier to stay two nights with the Wegners at Bad Honnef on the Rhine, before returning through Calais and the Channel Tunnel Exhibition near Folkestone to Birmingham on 10 August.

title: Avening Park, near Tetbury (last weekend in the van)
summary: A short autumn weekend camping trip (28-30 September 1990) — described as 'our last weekend in the van this year' — to Avening Park near Tetbury, Gloucestershire, stopping at Chalford en route to drop off a cello for repair. The family camped on the estate of Colonel Carter, walked into Avening village (lunching at the Cross pub), and strolled past Gatcombe Park (Princess Anne's home), picking blackberries and collecting conkers.

title: Cherry Tree Farm, Woodford Halse & Canons Ashby
summary: A short June 1990 family camping weekend at Cherry Tree Farm campsite near Woodford Halse, Northamptonshire, within view of the Daventry post office tower. The family camped in windy, showery weather (the boys' tent flooded overnight), walked into Woodford Halse for the church and supplies, and visited the National Trust house at Canons Ashby.

title: Di & Ian, Windmill Hill, Avebury, Devizes, Lacock, Bradford & Bath
summary: A spring weekend camping trip from Birmingham to Wiltshire and Somerset, visiting friends Di and Ian, then touring Windmill Hill, Avebury standing stones, West Kennet Long Barrow, Devizes, Lacock (Fox-Talbot museum), and Bradford-on-Avon, with overnight camps at Rowde and Stowford Farm near Trowbridge, ending with a visit to Bath's Roman Baths. John and Charles were left with grandparents; travellers were Simon, Eve, Thomas, William, and Peter.

title: Dinas, Welshpool Railway, Harlech & Barmouth with the Portals
summary: A Whitsun-week camping trip (30 May – 3 June 1990) from Birmingham to Dinas in Snowdonia, Wales, joined by Matt Portal and his children Claire and Peter. En route the family rode the Welshpool steam railway. Based at the Dinas campsite they made day trips to Harlech Castle and to Barmouth's arts-and-crafts village, aquarium and museums, then drove home via Llyn Celyn and the highest waterfall in Wales.

title: Hamperley & Long Mynd, north of Ludlow
summary: A short March 1990 break at a campsite at Hamperley in south Shropshire, near the Long Mynd. The family walked 5 miles across the Long Mynd (700 ft ascent), visited St Michael's church on Sunday, and drove out to the Boiling Well spring on the moorland. Friends Jini, Margaret and Helen joined them, as did Grandma and Grandpa.

title: Hook Norton, Wiggington & Sibford (late August 1990)
summary: A late August 1990 camping trip to the north Oxfordshire/south Warwickshire countryside, starting at a crowded pub campsite near Hook Norton before moving to a much-enjoyed wildfowl trust campsite at Wigginton for the weekend. The family explored the local villages, attended an evensong, enjoyed a barbecue, and on the Monday drove through Sibford for lunch before heading home to Birmingham.

title: New Forest, Beaulieu & Chandlers Ford (last trip of the year, November 1990)
summary: A late-season November 1990 domestic trip to the New Forest, with the family camping first at Roundhill and then Setthornes, visiting Beaulieu Motor Museum and walking near Lady Cross. On the return leg they stopped at Lymington market, stayed with friends ('Hattie') in Chandlers Ford, and paused in the Cotswolds to collect Simon's cello before arriving home.

title: Arlingham (St. Augustine's Farm) via Gloucester / Beauchamp House
summary: A short domestic trip on 10 August 1991 from Birmingham to Arlingham, Gloucestershire, stopping at Beauchamp House to watch Peter perform in the music-camp orchestra before arriving at St. Augustine's working farm to camp overnight. Family friend Kate Warburton joined the trip and babysat the younger children during the concert.

title: Short Autumn Outings: Trimley, Frensham & Kate and Jamie's Farm
summary: Three brief domestic overnight outings in autumn 1991 compressed onto a single diary page: a one-night stay at [?Trimley] in Suffolk on 31 August, an overnight camp at Frensham in Surrey on 7 September for Jane and Harry's wedding anniversary party, and a one-night visit to Kate and Jamie's farm on 22 September. All trips started and ended at home in Birmingham; no crossings were involved.

title: Dinas (Wales) via Wroxeter & Tallybont (Whitsun)
summary: A Whitsun family camping trip from Birmingham to a campsite at Dinas near Llanbedr in Gwynedd, Wales, stopping at the Roman ruins of Wroxeter on the way. On the Sunday the family attended church at Tal-y-bont, hosted the Hudfords for a barbeque, and went to the nearby tidal beach where the boys sailed Thomas' boat.

title: France 1991: Montreuil, Rouen & Chartres
summary: A July 1991 trip to northern France, departing Birmingham on 11 July and crossing the Channel by ferry from Dover. The family camped at Montreuil-sur-Mer and on the second day drove towards Chartres, stopping at Abbeville and visiting the cathedral city of Rouen, though they did not reach Chartres. The diary entry covers only the first two days; the rest of the trip is unrecorded in the surviving source.

title: France: Loire & Auvergne (Chartres, Chenonceaux, Conques, R. Lot)
summary: A July 1991 family camping holiday from Birmingham across the Channel to France, touring the Loire (Chartres, a Beauce windmill, Chaumont-sur-Loire, Chenonceau) before settling into lake bases at Vassivière and then a five-star site at Cassaniouze in the Auvergne. From Cassaniouze they made day trips to Conques, Estaing, Espalion and a kayak run on the River Lot from Entraygues. They returned north over the Pas de Peyrol via Vic-le-Comte, Roanne, Paris (Torcy), Beauvais and the Crécy battlefield before catching the ferry home on 30 July.

The van became badly bogged in the field on both Saturday and Sunday, requiring considerable effort and help from other campers to free it. On Sunday the family drove to Carding Mill Valley ("Card Mill") near Church Stretton for lunch before returning home.

title: Oxford / Folly Farm, Beckley (Easter); Chesham & Flamstead
summary: An Easter 1991 camping trip with six of the children (Thomas joining mid-trip after a music course at the Dragon School, Oxford). The family based themselves at Folly Farm, Beckley (Oxfordshire) for three nights, exploring Otmoor, Oddington and Oxford before moving to a valley campsite near Chesham for a final night. On the way home Tuesday they visited riding stables at Flamstead near Berkhamstead, where Charles rode a Shetland pony on his second birthday.

The exact location of Beauchamp House is unconfirmed but regional evidence (Trimpley near Bewdley, prior Cloverley Hall/Whitchurch journey) suggests Shropshire or Worcestershire.

title: Dinas (Wales) — Whitsun, with Harlech
summary: Whitsun 1992 camping trip from Birmingham to Dinas in Gwynedd, Wales, arriving 23 May after a slow drive through Wolverhampton and around Shrewsbury. The family spent four nights at a campsite near Dinas, making day excursions to Talybont church, the beach near Llanbedr, and an ancient coastal church, with a long walk towards the Rhinog hills on Tuesday. On the return on Wednesday 27 May they visited Harlech Castle, lunched at Trawsfynydd, and stopped at Llanfair to ride the narrow-gauge railway before heading home.

title: Ellesmere / Tetchill (new Igloo tent shakedown)
summary: A short Easter shakedown trip in April 1992 to test the family's new Igloo tent, ending up at a campsite in Tetchill near Ellesmere, Shropshire after being turned away from the Quinto campsite. The family stayed at least two nights, exploring Ellesmere town, visiting Ellesmere College, walking the canal, and attending church — despite a broken corkscrew, a faulty van heater, and overnight ice.

title: France 1992: Loire, Périgord & Conques
summary: Summer 1992 family camping holiday from Birmingham to France: a night near Lichfield, then a P&O ferry from Portsmouth to Le Havre. The family worked south through Normandy (Pont-Audemer), the Loire (Azay-le-Rideau, Richelieu with day trips to Chinon and Champigny-sur-Veude), the Périgord (St-Estèphe, Brantôme, Rouffignac and Les Eyzies caves, Rocamadour) to a fixed base at Cassaniouze, with excursions to Conques, Entraygues, Calvinet and Vieillevie. They returned north via Lac de Sarrans, Salbris, Chartres and St-Illiers-la-Ville (near Paris), then a ferry back to Southampton and home.

title: Lincoln & Hartsholme Country Park
summary: A long weekend (Friday 12 to Sunday 14 June 1992) camping at Hartsholme Country Park, 2 miles outside Lincoln, with the seven family members (Laura stayed home). They pitched the tent on the Friday evening, then bussed into Lincoln on Saturday to see the Stonebow gate, the Jew's House and the Cathedral, lunching at Pimento, and returned on Sunday morning for the main Cathedral service before driving home past the Fossedyke Roman canal.

summary: A weekend trip from Birmingham to the Peak District in September 1992, walking for three hours in Goyt Valley before visiting Buxton's museum, art gallery and The Crescent. The family camped overnight at King Sterndale, a village east of Buxton, joined by Siri the new German au pair.

title: York: Jorvik, Minster & Conisborough Castle
summary: A wet long-weekend break (23-25 October 1992) to York by camper van while Thomas and Peter were away singing in Paris and Chartres. Simon, Eve, William, John and Charles based at the Rowntree Park Caravan Site and explored York on foot - the Jorvik Centre, the Minster, the Treasurer's House, the National Railway Museum and the city walls. Heading home south on the Sunday they lunched near Pontefract and stopped to admire the circular white keep of Conisborough Castle.

On Saturday morning the rest of the family visited nearby Ashleworth Court to see the cruck barn and church, before the afternoon concert, after which they packed up and returned home.

title: Beauchamp music course & Forest of Dean
summary: A short two-day trip from Birmingham to a music summer course called 'Beauchamp' in or near the Forest of Dean, where Peter performed in string quartets and the family camped overnight. On the Saturday morning they visited the Forest of Dean Heritage Centre before returning home by 6 pm.

title: Cotswolds: Naunton, Guiting Power & Bourton-on-the-Water
summary: A weekend camping trip to the Cotswolds in late September 1993, with the family and German au pair Chris Goerdt camping behind a pub at Naunton. Day activities included a walk to Guiting Power on Saturday and a visit to the model village at Bourton-on-the-Water on the drive home Sunday.

title: Exmoor: Westermill Farm (Gawn & Polly's wedding)
summary: In September 1993 the family drove from Birmingham to Exmoor for Gawn & Polly Martin's wedding, basing themselves at Westermill Farm near Exford (after a tight squeeze over the narrow Silly Bridge). From the campsite they swam in the river, walked the moor, explored Dunster, and the parents attended the wedding at Dulverton Church with a reception at Withypool. On the Sunday they returned a guest to Taunton station and went blackberrying along the canal at Maunsell Lock before heading home.

title: France, Switzerland & Italy 1993: Guincourt, the St Gotthard, the Italian Lakes & Liguria
summary: A long July 1993 driving holiday from Birmingham through France, Switzerland and Italy and back. The family crossed Dover-Calais to Guincourt, drove via Alsace (Thann) over the St Gotthard Pass to the Italian Lakes (Porlezza, Lugano), then south through Lombardy and Emilia-Romagna (Cremona, the 'Arizona' campsite near Salsomaggiore, Parma/Torrechiara) to the Ligurian coast at Sestri Levante, with Cinque Terre excursions (Corniglia, Vernazza). They returned via Cuneo and the Alps to Les Deux Alpes, then Burgundy (Saint-Jean-de-Vaux, with suppers at Givry) before the long run north to Lumbres near Calais.

title: Italy/Switzerland 1993: via Ogbourne St George, Portsmouth, St Gotthard, Lugano, Cremona, Parma
summary: The trip title records an Easter 1993 camping journey to Italy and Switzerland (via the St Gotthard Pass, Lugano, Cremona and Parma), but the surviving transcribed pages for this spread (A_0086-A_0090) document only a UK loop from 16-20 April: south from Birmingham via a campsite at Ogbourne St George, then antique-shopping in Hungerford and stays at family/friends in Newbury and Chandlers Ford, with day visits to Winchester Cathedral, Romsey Rapids and Portsmouth Historic Dockyard (HMS Victory and the Mary Rose). The return leg runs through a Surrey campsite near Leatherhead/Horsley and a stop in London (Finsbury Circus) before a tiring drive home.

title: Manifold Valley & Market Drayton (Fair Oak, Broughton Hall)
summary: A short May Bank Holiday weekend trip in 1993 from Birmingham into Staffordshire and Shropshire. The family visited the Manifold Valley for a 3-hour bike ride on Saturday before camping at the Fair Oak campsite near Broughton Hall (a Franciscan convent) near Loggerheads and Market Drayton. On Sunday they attended a prayer-book Communion service in the old Broughton Hall chapel and walked 4.5 miles through the countryside before stopping at Wolverhampton on the way home.

title: North Wales: Rhiwiau Riding, Conwy
summary: A short North Wales trip on 7–8 August 1993, during which Thomas, William and Peter were deposited at Rhiwiau Riding Centre while Simon, Eve, John and Charles visited Conwy (walls, aquarium, smallest house in Britain) and camped near a dam somewhere in the hills. John and Charles walked 3½ miles into the hills the following morning.

title: Brecon Beacons family weekend: Blaensan, Ystradfellte & Tretower
summary: A late-May 1994 Whitsun bank-holiday weekend (Fri 27 to Tue 31 May) at a newly tried Welsh farm campsite, Blaensan, in the Brecon Beacons, with grandparents, an au pair and friends joining. From the fixed base the family walked to the Brecon Beacons Mountain Centre, drove over a pass to the Porth-yr-Ogof caves and Blue Pool at Ystradfellte, and on the departure day (Tuesday) saw Cwm Llwch and drove through Brecon and Bwlch (lunch with Joan Clarke) to Tretower Court and Castle before heading home to Birmingham.

This trip proper begins at the 'Friday May 27th' heading. Saltash Bridge, Ofculme and Dartington Cider Press are excluded from this trip's route.

title: Cornwall: Upton Festival, St Ives & the Lizard (Simon & Eve)
summary: A spring 1994 Cornwall touring holiday for just Simon and Eve. They set off from Birmingham via the Upton-upon-Severn music & arts festival (to hear Thomas in concert), overnighting near Welland, then drove down into Cornwall for a week of moving campsites: Altarnun, St Columb (Trekenning), St Ives (Ayr), St Just (Bosavern), Mullion Cove and Liskeard. Highlights included Delabole wind farm, Truro cathedral, the St Ives Tate and Hepworth galleries, the Geevor tin mine, Land's End, a walk around Lizard Point, and Trebah Garden, before crossing the Tamar at Saltash to head home.

title: France: Picardy, Champagne & Burgundy (Laon, Troyes, Vézelay, Autun, St Jean de Vaux)
summary: A summer 1994 family camping tour of northern and eastern France, drawn on a hand-sketched route map running Calais - St. Omer - Laon - Troyes - Vézelay - Morvan - Autun - Chalon-sur-Saône, with a return branch via Bar-sur-Aube and Clairvaux. After a concert at Snape and a night near Dover, the family crossed Dover-Calais and worked south through Picardy (Laon, the Chemin des Dames battlefields and Vauclair Abbey), Champagne (Troyes), and Burgundy, basing at campsites near Pontavert, Troyes, Arcy-sur-Cure (for Vézelay), and St-Jean-de-Vaux in the vineyards (revisiting Givry from the previous year). They returned north via Bar-sur-Aube, Clairvaux and Ste-Marie-du-Lac before the homeward leg.

title: Lee Valley (Chingford), London Tower & Cambridge
summary: A half-term autumn trip (26-29 October 1994) in which the whole family drove from Birmingham to the Lee Valley campsite at Chingford, where Eve, Peter and Charles were struck down by a stomach bug while Simon took Thomas, William and John by train into London to visit furniture shops and the Tower of London. The following day the family drove on to Cambridge, staying at a campsite at Great Shelford and visiting the colleges, Kettle's Yard and Evensong at King's before coming home a day early.

title: Lichfield & Cannock Chase day out
summary: A single-day family outing from Birmingham on Saturday 24 September 1994, visiting Lichfield Cathedral and the St. Mary's Heritage Centre before driving on to Cannock Chase for lunch and a walk on the common. No overnight stop; the party returned to Birmingham the same day.

title: Malvern Weekend – Shuttlefast Farm
summary: A short family weekend in July 1994: Simon, Eve, William, Peter, John and Charles drove from Birmingham to camp at Shuttlefast Farm near Malvern, detouring via Redditch to avoid motorway traffic. On Saturday they visited Malvern town, watched cricket and Wimbledon, and Thomas joined the family in the evening before all returning to the campsite.

title: North Wales: Llanfairfechan, Betws-y-Coed & Dolwyddelan (pony camp)
summary: An Easter half-term trip to North Wales in April 1994, with the family (minus Thomas, who was on a school French trip) driving from Birmingham to drop Peter and a friend Claire at a pony camp riding school near Dolwyddelan. On the way they stopped for lunch on the sea front at Llanfairfechan, visited the miniature railway museum at Betws-y-Coed, and camped overnight by the River Alwen. On the return leg (April 17) they attended church at Gwyddelwern, toured the ruins of Valle Crucis Abbey, and had lunch by the canal viaduct at Llangollen before collecting Thomas from Grandma's. A second diary entry for Friday 22 April records a return to Dolwyddelan to camp by the castle, suggesting a follow-up trip to collect Peter and Claire from pony camp.

boundary_flags.note: Friday 22nd April entry describes a second return to Dolwyddelan; this appears to be the start of a separate trip or continuation. The spread_range ends at A_0119 and boundary_mixed is true on this page.

title: North Wales: Roman Steps, Rhinog & Harlech
summary: A long-weekend camping trip to North Wales in early September 1994. The family based themselves near the Roman Steps in the Rhinog mountains for three nights, walking the Steps on Sunday morning before rain curtailed a planned Rhinog summit, and attending a Welsh Evensong at Harlech in the evening. The return journey on Tuesday included a stop at the Centre for Alternative Technology at Machynlleth.

title: Stourbridge Weekend (Parkland nr Stourbridge)
summary: A short June 1994 weekend trip from Birmingham, stopping briefly at a camping shop in Wolverhampton before settling overnight at an open parkland site approximately six miles from Stourbridge. The diary notes the children playing football and Charles crying, with no further day trips recorded.

title: Thorpeness & Naseby family gathering

The family left Birmingham on Monday 18 July, picking up Samra Kolon in the city centre and driving east on the just-opened A14 M1-A1 link road, by-passing Market Harborough and Kettering and breakfasting near Naseby. Simon drove home midweek and returned Friday; they set off home again on Sunday 24 July, picnicking west of Huntingdon.

title: Lake District: Coniston, Wast Water & Grasmere
summary: A short late-spring 1995 Lake District camping trip captured mid-stream: from a campsite at Coniston the family crossed Wrynose and Hard Knott passes (visiting Hardknott Roman Fort) to camp at Boot in Eskdale (Hollin Farm), explored Wast Water and St Olaf's church, then camped at South Wray Castle Farm. On Thursday 4 May they visited Dove Cottage at Grasmere and Townend at Troutbeck before setting off home to Birmingham at 4.30.

They overnighted at Foxcovert Farm near Knutsford en route, then at Hawkshead, then settled for multiple nights at Pier Cottage on Lake Coniston, making a day trip by steam yacht to Ruskin's Brantwood. Further stops including Eskdale and Wast Water are implied by the trip title but are not yet documented in the transcribed pages.

title: London: Pickett's Lock, V&A & British Museum
summary: A three-day family trip (Friday–Monday, 22–24 October 1995) to London, based at Pickett's Lock leisure centre in Edmonton. The seven family members visited the V&A, British Museum, London Planetarium, Westminster Abbey, Regent's Park, Liberty's and the Adelphi, and attended evening cinema screenings at the campsite.

title: Spain: Picos de Europa, Santiago, Galicia, Portugal & Salamanca
summary: In late July 1995 Simon, Eve and four sons (William was away in the USA) drove from Birmingham to Southampton and took a night ferry to northern Spain. They toured the Cantabrian coast and Picos de Europa (Castro Urdiales, Santillana del Mar, Covadonga), crossed via the Ponton Pass to Leon and the Sanabria area, then followed the pilgrim route through O Cebreiro and Portomarin to Santiago de Compostela and the Galician coast at Leis. After a loop into northern Portugal (Braga, Porto, Castro Daire) they returned to Spain through Ciudad Rodrigo, La Alberca, Salamanca, Tordesillas and the Soria/Rioja highlands, finishing at Najera before sailing Bilbao to Portsmouth and driving home, arriving 14 Aug 1995.

title: Beauchamp (Gloucester): John's Cello Course
summary: A short April 1996 camping weekend (9–15 April) based at Beauchamp Farm near Over, Gloucestershire, where eight-year-old John attended his first cello course. Simon, Eve, Thomas, William, John and Charles (Peter was separately in France) camped in the field and made day trips to Gloucester Cathedral, the Docks, a swimming pool and the remarkable Saxon sites of Odda's Chapel and St Mary's Deerhurst. The week ended with John's concert on April 15th, where he performed ‘Greensleeves’ and ‘The Great Western’ in the senior orchestra.

title: Doncaster (puppy) / Appleby Magna day trip
summary: A Sunday day trip on 16 June 1996, collecting Peter from church and driving north to see a litter of puppies near Doncaster, where the family chose their new dog. Tea was taken at Appleby Magna on the return journey to Birmingham.

title: Farnborough Hall (day trip)
summary: A one-day family outing on 25 May 1996, substituting for a planned trip to Sinai after Peter was ill for four weeks. The family drove from Birmingham to Farnborough Hall in Warwickshire (six miles from Banbury), visiting the painted rooms, library, and china collections, before returning home after tea at 4:30pm.

title: Ireland: Glendalough, Kilkenny, Cashel, Dingle & the Blaskets
summary: August 1996 family camping tour of Ireland: ferry from Holyhead to Dún Laoghaire, then a clockwise loop via Glendalough, Kildare, Kilkenny, the Rock of Cashel and the Vale of Aherlow to the Dingle Peninsula, with a boat trip to the Great Blasket. They crossed the Shannon car ferry to Clare for the Cliffs of Moher, the Burren and Aillwee Cave, then turned inland via Clonmacnoise and Lough Ree before finishing at a family christening in Killyleagh, Co Down, returning home via Knowth and the Dún Laoghaire–Holyhead ferry.

title: France 1997: Amiens, Loire, Bourges, Conques & the Gorges du Tarn
summary: A summer 1997 family camping trip from Birmingham via the Eurotunnel Shuttle into northern France, dropping the French exchange Loic at Amiens and visiting Amiens Cathedral. The family worked south through Loeuilly, L'Isle-Adam, Paris (Eiffel Tower), La Ferté-Saint-Aubin and Preuilly (with day trips to Bourges cathedral and the Palais Jacques-Coeur), then settled at a Cassaniouze base for visits to Conques, Calvinet and Vieillevie canoeing. They finished in the Gorges du Tarn region, taking in the Aven Armand caves, La Malène boat trips, Sainte-Énimie and a night at Monastier.

title: France 1997: Burgundy & the journey home
summary: The closing leg of the family's France 1997 touring holiday, 12-16 August 1997. On 15 August they drove north past Reims to La Belle Pêche at Hames-Boucres near Calais, crossed back to England by Eurotunnel le shuttle on 16 August, and reached home in Birmingham despite a puncture by Oxford on the M40.

title: Malmesbury: Burton Hill Family Weekend
summary: On Saturday 6 September 1997, the family drove from Birmingham to Burton Hill near Malmesbury, Wiltshire, listening to Princess Diana's funeral on the radio. title: Germany 2003: Bad Honnef, the Rhine, Alsfeld, Eisenach & Weimar
summary: In August 2003 Simon, Eve and three of their sons (Thomas, John and Charles) drove from Birmingham via an overnight at Ashford in Kent and the Channel Tunnel, through France, Belgium and Holland into Germany. They stayed with friends at Bad Honnef on the Rhine, then worked east and south through Heimertshausen/Alsfeld, Eisenach (Wartburg Castle, Luther & Bach houses), Erfurt and Weimar (Bauhaus, Goethe, Schiller), before turning back via Buchenwald, Bamberg, Rothenburg ob der Tauber, Rot am See and Heidelberg. The return ran through Hermeskeil, Neufchateau in the Belgian Ardennes and Ypres/Kemmel, reaching home around Saturday 16 August.

The window closes heading north via Guignicourt to the Fontinettes boat-lift near St-Omer, before the return home (covered in the next diary window).

title: Spain & France 2008: Bilbao Ferry, Burgos, Zamora, Salamanca, Madrid & the Pyrenees
summary: A long summer 2008 tour of Spain and France by Simon, Eve and their son Charles (19), departing Birmingham on Sunday 27 July. They drove south via Savernake Forest to a friend's house in Wiltshire, then took an overnight Bay of Biscay ferry (P&O Portsmouth-Bilbao) while Thomas, Naomi and Austin drove their own van over the Pyrenees. Landing near Bilbao, the family toured Castile and León — Burgos, Zamora (with San Pedro de la Nave), Salamanca, the Wellington battlefield at Arapiles, Ávila, Toledo and Madrid (staying with the Triana family) — following Wellington's Peninsular War campaign and visiting lakeside campsites in the Sierra de Urbión. title: France, Switzerland & Italy: Vitry-le-François, Gadmen & Pavia
summary: A June 2022 motorhome/cycling tour from Birmingham via a Kent campsite and the Channel into France, with overnight stops near Vitry-le-François and Les Hôpitaux-Neufs (St Point Lake), then over into Switzerland to camp at Gadmen. The family crossed the Susten Pass and St Gotthard Tunnel into Italy, settling for several days at Pavia (cathedral, San Michele, San Pietro in Ciel d'Oro, the Visconti castle and Certosa monastery) before moving to the rural Camping Relax at Pian del Voglio in the Apennines.
